content:
Did you know that Earth is like a big spinning top? It spins around while moving in a circle around the Sun. But here’s the cool part: Earth is tilted a little bit, like it’s leaning to one side. Because of this tilt, different parts of Earth get different amounts of sunlight during the year.

When your home is tilted toward the Sun, it feels warm and sunny—that’s summer! When it’s tilted away, it’s cooler and darker—that’s winter. In between, we get spring and fall, when the weather changes between warm and cold.

So, the seasons happen because Earth is spinning and leaning as it goes around the Sun. That’s why you see snow in winter and fun sunshine in summer. It’s like Earth’s way of making the year exciting and different!
